plain_definition: The federal statute that created the United States' first dedicated legal framework for 'payment stablecoins.' Enacted 18 July 2025 as Public Law 119-27, it establishes a licensing regime under which only a 'permitted payment stablecoin issuer' -- a bank subsidiary approved under the Act, a 'Federal qualified payment stablecoin issuer' approved by the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency, or a 'State qualified payment stablecoin issuer' approved by a state regulator -- may lawfully issue a payment stablecoin in the United States, subject to reserve, redemption, disclosure, and examination requirements.
formal_definition: Public Law 119-27, 139 Stat. 419 (approved July 18, 2025). Section 1 (12 U.S.C. 5901 note) sets the statute's short title as the Guiding and Establishing National Innovation for U.S. Stablecoins Act, or GENIUS Act. Section 2 (12 U.S.C. 5901) contains the Act's operative definitions, including 'payment stablecoin' (section 2(22)) and 'permitted payment stablecoin issuer' (section 2(23)).
